explanation: The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous requirement for both actions to be performed by the system. The 'shall' indicates a global property, meaning it must always hold. Therefore, the LTL property states that globally (G), the system measures the breathing rate (measureRR) AND displays the breathing rate (displayRR) simultaneously.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous and mandatory capability of the system. The atomic propositions 'measureRR' (indicates the system measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (indicates the system displays the breathing rate) directly correspond to the actions described. The 'G' (globally) operator is used to ensure that these two actions are always simultaneously true, reflecting the persistent nature of "shall measure and display."

The natural language statement requires the system to continuously measure and display the breathing rate. The 'shall' implies a global requirement. Therefore, the LTL property states that it is globally true that the system measures the breathing rate (measureRR) AND displays the breathing rate (displayRR).

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous requirement for both actions to occur. The 'shall' keyword indicates an invariant property. Therefore, the LTL property uses the global operator 'G' to ensure that at all times, the system measures the breathing rate (measureRR) AND displays the breathing rate (displayRR). The '&' operator ensures that both conditions must hold simultaneously.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous and concurrent relationship between measuring and displaying the breathing rate. The LTL property captures this by stating that globally (G), if the system is in a state where it is measuring the breathing rate (measureRR), then it must also simultaneously be in a state where it is displaying the breathing rate (displayRR). This ensures that whenever the measurement function is active, the corresponding display function is also active.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous requirement for both actions. The LTL property G (measureRR & displayRR) translates this by stating that globally (G), both the measurement of the breathing rate (measureRR) and the display of the breathing rate (displayRR) must always be true. The logical AND (&) ensures that both conditions are met simultaneously.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous and persistent requirement for the system to perform both actions. The LTL operator 'G' (globally) signifies that a property must hold true at all times. The atomic propositions 'measureRR' (the system measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (the system displays the breathing rate) represent the two actions. Combining them with the logical AND operator '&' ensures that both measurement and display are concurrently active. Therefore, 'G (measureRR & displayRR)' accurately captures that, at every point in time, the system must be both measuring and displaying the breathing rate.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous requirement for both actions to occur. The atomic proposition 'measureRR' directly represents the act of measuring the breathing rate, while 'displayRR' represents displaying it. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures that this condition holds true at all times in the system's execution. The '&' (AND) operator logically connects 'measureRR' and 'displayRR', enforcing that both actions must simultaneously or continuously take place.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous requirement. The LTL property `G (measureRR & displayRR)` captures this by stating that globally (G), the system must always be both measuring the breathing rate (`measureRR`) and displaying the breathing rate (`displayRR`). This ensures that the two actions are perpetually active as mandated by the 'shall' keyword.

The natural language statement "The system shall measure and display the breathing rate" implies a continuous requirement. The LTL operator 'G' (Globally) is used to express that a condition must always hold true. The phrase "measure and display" indicates that both actions, measuring the breathing rate and displaying it, must occur simultaneously or be continuously maintained. Therefore, the LTL formula uses the 'G' operator applied to the conjunction ('&') of 'measureRR' (measures the breathing rate) and 'displayRR' (displays the breathing rate), ensuring that at all times, the system is both measuring and displaying the breathing rate.
